query: Ursula von der Leyen proposed new EU Commission team Commission II commissioners list December 2024

mako.raw:
{"query":"Ursula von der Leyen proposed new EU Commission team Commission II commissioners list December 2024","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1667,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://commission.europa.eu/about/organisation/college-commissioners_en","title":"College of Commissioners - European Commission","content":"The College is composed of Commissioners from 27 EU countries.
Together, these 27 members of the College are appointed as the Commission's political leadership for a 5-year term."},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Von_der_Leyen_Commission_II","title":"Von der Leyen Commission II - Wikipedia","content":"The proposed College includes five returning commissioners: three of them are for a second mandate (Šuica, Várhelyi, Hoekstra), while Dombrovskis would return for a third term, and Šefčovič for a fourth term.
On 19 September 2024, the Council of the European Union adopted, by common accord with the President-elect of the Commission, the list of persons whom it proposes for appointment as members of the Commission to the European Parliament.
This corresponds to a 51 % majority of all members, the least supported European Commission by the Parliament since 1993, when it was given the right to vote on the college, and fewer than the 401 MEPs who voted for the election of Ursula von der Leyen as Commission President.
It subsequently assumed office on 1 December 2024.
The policy priorities of the second von der Leyen Commission were initially outlined in the 30-page "Political Guidelines for the Next European Commission 2024–2029" document that Ursula von der Leyen published in July 2024.
In November 2024, Vice-President for Clean, Just and Competitive Transition, Teresa Ribera, expressed support for the European Green Deal and the green transition.
On 4 March 2025, Ursula von der Leyen announced the EU's €800 billion (US$840 billion) defence investment plan "ReArm Europe".
Following these discussions, on 26 June 2026, the European Commission formally proposed extending temporary protection for displaced Ukrainians until March 2028.
Archived from the original on 18 June 2024.
"The Formation of the Second von der Leyen Commission: Nomination, Organisation and Confirmation"."},{"url":"https://epthinktank.eu/2026/03/07/the-policy-priorities-of-the-von-der-leyen-ii-commission-state-of-play-in-march-2026/","title":"The policy priorities of the von der Leyen II Commission – State of play in March 2026 | Epthinktank | European Parliament","content":"This EPRS paper analyses how the European Commission is delivering on the policy agenda set by its President, Ursula von der Leyen, and her College of Commissioners since taking office in December 2024.
The von der Leyen II Commission has set seven priorities – different from the six priorities of her first mandate.
It has so far announced close to 400 initiatives."},{"url":"https://commission.europa.eu/about/commission-2024-2029_en","title":"Commission 2024-2029 - European Commission","content":"President Ursula von der Leyen, who has led the Commission since 2019, started a second mandate with a new team of Commissioners on 1 December 2024.
The President, together with the new College of Commissioners, has defined the priorities of the European Commission for 2024-2029, based on the Political Guidelines presented to the European Parliament on the day of her election."},{"url":"https://www.foederalist.eu/p/commission-2024.html","title":"Der (europäische) Föderalist: The 2024 EU Commission","content":"Between the end of the (informal) nomination period on 30 August and the public presentation of the college of commissioners in mid-September, von der Leyen has therefore been putting pressure on some governments to reconsider their proposals and put forward a woman after all.
The PfE, ESN and Left groups voted unanimously against the confirmation.
FI (EPP) — On 30 August, the Italian government officially proposed EU minister Fitto (FdI/ECR) as commissioner."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Ursula von der Leyen proposed new EU Commission team Commission II commissioners list December 2024","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Von_der_Leyen_Commission_II","title":"Von der Leyen Commission II - Wikipedia","content":"| 8th Commission of the European Union |. | President of the Commission | **Ursula von der Leyen** (EPP, GER) |. The **second von der Leyen Commission** is the current European Commission, in office since 1 December 2024. It consists of one commissioner from each of the member states of the European Union – including Ursula von der Leyen, its president, who is from Germany. The Commission was approved by the European Parliament on 27 November with 51% of the majority, the least supported European Commission by the Parliament since 1993, when it was given the right to vote on the college. Regardless of the three-level structure of the commission, the president of the commission emphasised that all commissioners share collective responsibility for decisions made by the Commission. | President of the European Commission | **Ursula von der Leyen** | EPP (CDU) | Germany | SG, SJ, COMM | 1 December 2024 |  |.","score":0.8396422,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://europeannewsroom.com/europes-next-chapter-von-der-leyens-commission-team-unveiled-for-new-term","title":"Europe’s next chapter: Von der Leyen’s Commission team unveiled for new term | European Newsroom","content":"# Europe’s next chapter: Von der Leyen’s Commission team unveiled for new term. Ursula von der Leyen presented her candidates for the future EU Commission. Ursula von der Leyen, President of the European Commission, presents her new Commission to media representatives during a press conference. After weeks of speculation and backdoor negotiations, on Tuesday the President of the European Commission, **Ursula von der Leyen**, presented her team of European commissioners. On the staff front, the appointment of the right-wing populist **Raffaele Fitto**, a member of Italian Prime Minister **Giorgia Meloni**’s Fratelli d’Italia party, as one of the vice-presidents of the EU Commission remains particularly contentious. ”But this time, for example, the topic of security, triggered by the Russian war in Ukraine, but also the topic of competitiveness, has much more influence” on the organisation of her team, von der Leyen said. Von der Leyen has filled many of the top jobs in her new Commission team with women.","score":0.6504012,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.foederalist.eu/p/commission-2024.html","title":"Der (europäische) Föderalist: The 2024 EU Commission","content":"It is a European election year, and that means not only the election of a new European Parliament in June, but also of a new European Commission in the autumn. However, unlike in any parliamentary democracy, the right of nomination lies with the Council, which in turn adopts the list of proposed commissioners “by common accord with the [Commission] President-elect” and “on the basis of the suggestions made by Member States” (Art. 17 (7) (2) TEU). But if MEPs reject a candidate, the replacement commissioner is also proposed by the national government. Governments wishing to secure an attractive policy area for their nominee are therefore well advised to consult with Ursula von der Leyen (CDU/EPP) in advance, who was re-elected as Commisison president on 18 July. Between the end of the (informal) nomination period on 30 August and the public presentation of the college of commissioners in mid-September, von der Leyen has therefore been putting pressure on some governments to reconsider their proposals and put forward a woman after all.","score":0.6194488,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://euneighbourseast.eu/news/latest-news/von-der-leyen-unveils-proposed-new-european-commission-team","title":"Von der Leyen unveils proposed new European ...","content":"The new 27-member Commission will include six executive vice-presidents (EVPs) – four women and two men. Among the vice-presidents is Estonia's former prime","score":0.5689194,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://english.aawsat.com/world/5061828-von-der-leyen-names-european-commissions-new-top-team","title":"Von der Leyen Names European Commission's New Top ...","content":"Ursula von der Leyen on Tuesday named Spain's ecological transition minister Teresa Ribera as the bloc's next antitrust commissioner","score":0.5313802,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.26,"request_id":"36b58ef5-4def-4aec-ac1d-6c75ed4d28be"}
